response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial experience (founder of an open-source Geo-Data/GIS platform used by Green Map) and has built low-latency, high-throughput systems handling millions of requests per second using DLang with async I/O and caching, which maps conceptually to the pipeline performance work in this role. However, their professional C++ experience is dated (LabWindows/CVI components early in their career), they have no Rust experience, and their recent work is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js full-stack product development. They also lack direct routing/navigation-systems experience, which is an explicit requirement.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data experience via a self-built open-source GIS/map platform in production use
Proven low-latency distributed systems work (async I/O, caching, fibers) at millions of requests/sec
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, Linux, Docker, Kubernetes, and testing tooling authorship
concerns: Modern C++ (or Rust) is the primary requirement, but their C/C++ work is old and limited; recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js
No experience building navigation or routing guidance systems specifically
Career trajectory skews toward full-stack/web product work rather than large-scale data pipeline engineering
